title: Filtered latent tyre-wear state via simple state-space/Kalman features
summary: A new 2026 F1 tyre-degradation paper models wear as a latent state with pit-stop resets and finds better sequential prediction than an AR benchmark.
rationale: A new 2026 F1 tyre-degradation paper models wear as a latent state with pit-stop resets and finds better sequential prediction than an AR benchmark. That is a more concrete version of the broad ‘latent tyre state’ idea: instead of static degradation ratios, estimate hidden wear level and wear slope from past lap-time observations only.
implementation_hint: For each `Race_Year-Driver` sequence, fit a lightweight past-only filter with state components like latent pace loss and degradation slope; reset state after `PitStop=1`. Export filtered state mean, slope, innovation, and uncertainty as tabular features for the existing classifier.
expected_effect: Better separation between normal long stints and true cliff/stop-trigger moments, especially when raw `LapTime_Delta` is noisy.
risk: Model misspecification on wet races or traffic-heavy laps can inject noise; computational setup is more complex than ordinary feature engineering.
